label: Advanced Item API
title: Advanced Item API
bodyHtml:
When to use this page

The basic Item API page uses vanilla ItemInfo to mimic the base game. Advanced items can use CustomItemInfo, which includes normal ItemInfo fields, vanilla LiquidItemInfo fields, and CUCoreLib-only fields like Container, Battery, Light, Tool, WornSprite, WornSpriteOffset, SpriteScaleDimensions, SpawnFrequency, and CustomData.

Why is the mod doing it this way? Traditonally, the game sets these settings via the Unity prefab editor, and as such does not appear in the game's default item code.

// Replace new ItemInfo{ ... } with 
      new CustomItemInfo{ ... }

Modded-only fields

CustomItemInfo keeps vanilla fields like fullName, category, weight, tags, and useAction. It also exposes vanilla liquid-container fields like capacity, defaultContents, and autoFill. The fields below are CUCoreLib-only data. The base game does not know about them unless CUCoreLib reads them and applies the matching runtime behavior.

Field Type What CUCoreLib uses it for
IDstringFilled by ItemRegistry.Register from the separate ID argument. You normally do not set this yourself.
IconSpriteInventory/world sprite used by custom templates and cached under the item ID. Passing the sprite as the third register argument sets this for you.
WornSpriteSpriteOptional sprite applied while a wearable custom item is worn, then restored to Icon when dropped.
WornSpriteOffsetVector2Optional local-space offset applied to the worn item sprite after vanilla attaches it to desiredWearLimb.
SpriteScalefloatScale applied to the generated runtime template. Keep this near 1f unless your art was made at a different size.
SpriteScaleDimensionsSpriteScaleDimensionsScales the sprite toward a target pixel size like (14f, 14f). Add true as the third tuple value to stop once either axis reaches the requested size instead of forcing both axes to meet it.
SpawnFrequencyintLoot pool injection weight. 0 means craft-only, 1 is the normal default, higher values make it more common.
ContainerContainerPropertiesAdds/configures a vanilla Container component on spawned items.
BatteryBatteryPropertiesAdds/configures a vanilla BatteryItem component on spawned items.
LightLightPropertiesAdds/configures a Light2D emitter and optionally wires it to vanilla LightItem behavior.
BandageBandagePropertiesInstalls a vanilla-style BandageMinigame limb action and applies limb healing, pain reduction, and bandage slow values.
SyringeSyringePropertiesAdds syringe-style liquid injection behavior through WaterContainerItem and SyringeMinigame.
ToolToolPropertiesBuilds a vanilla AttackInfo and calls Body.Attack for melee-style tools or weapons.
SpawnComponentsList<string>Qualified MonoBehaviour type names CUCoreLib adds to the spawned item GameObject the first time the item appears. For plugin-defined scripts, use the assembly-qualified form like "YourNamespace.YourClass, YourModDll".
CustomDataDictionary<string, object>Registration-time metadata for your own mod code. Read it later with ItemRegistry.TryGetCustomData<T>.

LiquidItemInfo fields

Use these direct fields for normal liquid containers like bottles, cans, canteens, pouches, and drinkable items. Use SyringeProperties only when you want the syringe minigame and injection action.

FieldTypeWhat it does
capacityfloatMaximum liquid capacity in mL.
defaultContentsList<LiquidStack>Liquids added to freshly spawned items.
autoFillboolWhether a loose item can fill itself from world liquid tiles when it has space.

ContainerProperties

FieldTypeWhat it does
CapacityfloatMaximum total held weight for the vanilla Container.
MaxWeightPerItemfloatMaximum weight any one contained item may have.
EncumbranceReductionfloatMultiplier for how much contained weight counts against the player. 1f is normal; 0.5f feels half as heavy.

BatteryProperties

FieldTypeWhat it does
MaxChargefloatMaximum charge stored by the item once a battery is loaded.
StartChargefloatStarting charge amount used to initialize item condition on fresh custom items.
PresetBatteryItem.BatteryPresetVanilla preset for accepted battery size: Small, Medium, or Large.
BatteryTypestringItem ID for the battery installed by default, such as smallbattery, mediumbattery, or largebattery.
SpawnWithBatteryboolIf true, fresh items start with BatteryType already loaded. If false, the item starts empty.

BandageProperties

FieldTypeWhat it does
EffectivenessfloatDivisor for minigame use amount. Higher values make the item last longer.
SkinHealAmountfloatSkin healing applied per use amount.
BandageSlowAmountfloatBleeding slowdown applied per use amount.
PainReductionfloatPain removed per use amount.
BoneHealTimerReductionfloatBroken-bone recovery timer reduction per use amount.
DislocationTimerReductionfloatDislocation timer reduction per use amount.
MinigameColorColorColor passed into the vanilla bandage minigame.
CreateWrapSpriteboolIf true, CUCoreLib creates a temporary limb wrap sprite when used.
WrapSpritePathstringResource path for the temporary wrap sprite.
WrapSpriteColorColorTint for the temporary wrap sprite.

SyringeProperties

This is for syringe-style limb injection, not general bottle/canteen liquid storage.

FieldTypeWhat it does
CapacityfloatMaximum liquid capacity in mL.
AutoFillboolWhether the added WaterContainerItem auto-fills when supported by vanilla behavior.
AmountPerFullUsefloatLiquid amount injected when the minigame multiplier is 1f.
UseAverageColorboolIf true, the minigame color comes from the contained liquid average.
MinigameColorColorFallback minigame color when UseAverageColor is false.
DefaultContentsList<LiquidStack>Liquids added to fresh spawned items.

ToolProperties

FieldTypeWhat it does
DamagefloatDamage against animals or living targets.
StructuralDamagefloatDamage against blocks, buildings, and non-animal structures.
AttackCooldownMultiplierfloatMultiplier applied to vanilla attack cooldown after hitting animals.
DistancefloatRaycast reach for the attack.
KnockBackfloatImpulse applied through vanilla attack knockback.
CooldownfloatBase attack cooldown.
AttackAnimationstringResource name for the swing animation prefab, usually SwingAnim.
StaminaUsefloatStamina spent when swinging.
PiercingboolIf true, vanilla attack raycasts can continue through hits.
SwingSoundsstring[]Sound IDs randomly chosen when swinging.
VolumefloatSwing sound volume.
RotateAmountfloatVisual attack rotation amount.
PhysicalSwingboolIf true, vanilla arm power, exertion, and physical swing effects apply.
DoAttackAnimationboolIf true, vanilla arms swing animation plays.
MetalMoreDamageboolIf true, vanilla metallic target bonus damage applies.
ConditionLossOnHitfloatItem condition subtracted after a successful hit.

LightProperties

FieldTypeWhat it does
IntensityfloatBrightness of the generated Light2D.
ColorColorLight tint. Defaults to white.
PointLightOuterRadiusfloatOuter radius for point lights.
PointLightInnerRadiusfloatInner radius for point lights.
LightTypeCustomLightTypeCUCoreLib light type mapped internally to URP Light2D.LightType. Use CustomLightType.Point for lantern-style item light.
OffsetVector2Local offset for the light object relative to the item.
AddLightItemboolIf true, CUCoreLib wires the light through vanilla LightItem so container/on-off behavior can apply.
 
ItemRegistry.Register(
    "SomeSlightlyMoreAdvancedItem",
    new CustomItemInfo
    {
        fullName = "someName",
        // ... and other ItemInfo fields...

        // Cool CUCoreLib-only fields!
        SpawnFrequency = 0,
        SpriteScale = 1f,
        CustomData =
        {
            ["family"] = "utility-bag"
        } 
        // etc...
    },
    AssetLoader.LoadEmbeddedSprite("Images.someSweetPixelArt.png")
);

Bandages

This is a bit different then the decompiled code. BandageProperties starts a BandageMinigame, converts the minigame angle into a use amount, reduces item condition, then adds limb healing and bleeding slowdown values.

ItemRegistry.Register(
    "clothpatch",
    new CustomItemInfo
    {
        fullName = "Cloth patch",
        description = "A small patch of clean cloth.",
        category = "medical",
        tags = "medicine,dressing,cangetwet",
        weight = 0.05f,
        value = 3,
        rec = new Recognition(3),
        Bandage = new BandageProperties
        {
            Effectiveness = 8f,
            SkinHealAmount = 8f,
            BandageSlowAmount = 18f,
            PainReduction = 40f,
            BoneHealTimerReduction = 5f,
            DislocationTimerReduction = 5f,
            MinigameColor = new Color(0.9f, 0.9f, 0.9f)
        },
        SpawnFrequency = 1
    },
    AssetLoader.LoadEmbeddedSprite("Images.clothpatch.png")
);

Syringes

SyringeProperties makes the item behave like a liquid syringe: CUCoreLib adds WaterContainerItem, starts SyringeMinigame, then calls wat.Inject(limb, amount) using the minigame multiplier.

ItemRegistry.Register(
    "reliefinjector",
    new CustomItemInfo
    {
        fullName = "Relief injector",
        description = "A small injector prefilled with relief cream.",
        category = "medicine",
        tags = "medicine",
        slotRotation = -45f,
        destroyAtZeroCondition = false,
        combineable = true,
        scaleWeightWithCondition = true,
        weight = 0.25f,
        value = 8,
        rec = new Recognition(5),
        Syringe = new SyringeProperties
        {
            Capacity = 100f,
            AmountPerFullUse = 100f,
            AutoFill = false,
            // Not sure what this is? Read the next page for liquid documentation.
            DefaultContents =
            {
                new LiquidStack("reliefcream", 100f)
            }
        },
        SpawnFrequency = 1
    },
    AssetLoader.LoadEmbeddedSprite("Images.reliefinjector.png")
);

The liquid decides what injection actually does. WaterContainerItem.Inject drains the liquid stack and calls the liquid's health-use behavior when that liquid is injectable.

You'll learn more about liquids and their use on the Liquids page.

Containers

Containers are runtime components, not just item stats. CUCoreLib's container module adds or configures a vanilla Container component when the item starts.

Capacity maps to the container's maximum held weight. MaxWeightPerItem limits individual items. EncumbranceReduction maps to the vanilla encumbrance multiplier: 1f means normal weight, 0.5f means the contents feel half as heavy.

ItemRegistry.Register(
    "cinderstalkbag",
    new CustomItemInfo
    {
        fullName = "Cinderstalk Bag",
        description = "A compact all-natural bag. Who says plants can't be stylish?",
        category = "custom",
        weight = 1.2f,
        value = 18,
        tags = "cangetwet",
        rec = new Recognition(6),
        Container = new ContainerProperties
        {
            Capacity = 5f,
            MaxWeightPerItem = 2f,
            EncumbranceReduction = 0.65f
        },
        SpawnFrequency = 1
    },
    AssetLoader.LoadEmbeddedSprite("Images.cinderstalk-bag.png")
);

Battery-powered items

Battery-powered tools use the vanilla BatteryItem component. CUCoreLib's battery module adds one and sets its maximum charge. Use this when the item itself stores charge or accepts battery behavior; use vanilla BatteryInfo when you are registering an actual battery item.

ItemRegistry.Register(
    "portablelamp",
    new CustomItemInfo
    {
        fullName = "Portable lamp",
        description = "A battery-fed lamp for the fuzzy cave explorer.",
        category = "utility",
        tags = "belttool",
        usable = true,
        value = 22,
        weight = 0.8f,
        rec = new Recognition(5),
        decayMinutes = 180f,
        decayInfo = (byte)ItemInfo.DecayType.BatteryDecay,
        Battery = new BatteryProperties
        {
            MaxCharge = 100f,
            StartCharge = 35f,
            Preset = BatteryItem.BatteryPreset.Medium,
            BatteryType = "mediumbattery"
        },
        Light = new LightProperties
        {
            Intensity = 0.75f,
            Color = Color.white,
            PointLightOuterRadius = 7.5f,
            PointLightInnerRadius = 0f,
            LightType = CustomLightType.Point
        },
        SpawnFrequency = 1
    },
    AssetLoader.LoadEmbeddedSprite("Images.portablelamp.png")
);

Non-gun weapons

For melee tools and small weapons, ToolProperties builds a vanilla AttackInfo and calls Body.Attack. This gives custom tools the same swing, raycast, hit sound, cooldown, damage-number, and block-damage path used by vanilla tools.

ItemRegistry.Register("glassshiv", new CustomItemInfo
{
    fullName = "Glass shiv",
    description = "A sharpened piece of glass wrapped in cloth.",
    category = "weapon",
    tags = "tool,cangetwet,cutting",
    weight = 0.25f,
    value = 4,
    rec = new Recognition(3),
    Tool = new ToolProperties
    {
        Damage = 22f,
        StructuralDamage = 8f,
        AttackCooldownMultiplier = 0.7f,
        Distance = 4.6f,
        KnockBack = 120f,
        Cooldown = 0.28f,
        StaminaUse = 0.35f,
        Piercing = true,
        ConditionLossOnHit = 0.08f
    }
}, AssetLoader.LoadEmbeddedSprite("Images.glassshiv.png"), 1);

Equippables

Wearables are regular items with wearable fields set. wearSlotId is the save/load and replacement key. desiredWearLimb points the visual/armor behavior at a body region, while wearableArmor and wearableIsolation affect protection and temperature.

 ItemRegistry.Register(
     "fieldpack",
     new CustomItemInfo
     {
         fullName = "Field Pack",
         description = "A versatile pack for carrying supplies.",
         category = "tool",
         wearable = true,
         wearableCanBeHeld = true,
         desiredWearLimb = "UpTorso",
         wearSlotId = "back",
         wearableArmor = 0.18f,
         wearableIsolation = 0.08f,
         wearableHitDurabilityLossMultiplier = 0.7f,
         weight = 1.1f,
         value = 14,
         tags = "cangetwet",
         rec = new Recognition(4),
         WornSprite = AssetLoader.LoadEmbeddedSprite("Images.fieldpack-worn.png"),
         WornSpriteOffset = new Vector2(0f, -0.04f),
         Container = new ContainerProperties
         {
             Capacity = 8f,
             MaxWeightPerItem = 3f,
             EncumbranceReduction = 0.5f
         },
         SpawnFrequency = 1
     },
     AssetLoader.LoadEmbeddedSprite("Images.fieldpack.png")
 );

In this example, we combined both the prior container tag with the new wearable fields to make a backpack that also functions as a container.

A lot of more unique items such as this can be made with combinations of tags. Give it a try!

Decay bit flags

decayInfo is a byte, but it represents bit flags from ItemInfo.DecayType. Combine flags with bitwise OR (|). The enums are: NoDecayWithoutContainerItem (1), NoDecayWhenNotWorn (2), NoDecayWhenStill (4), and BatteryDecay (16).

decayMinutes = 240f,
decayInfo = (byte)(
    ItemInfo.DecayType.NoDecayWhenNotWorn | ItemInfo.DecayType.NoDecayWhenStill
);

BatteryDecay (16) changes decay from normal item condition loss into battery drain. Use it for battery-powered items that should consume charge over time instead of rotting or breaking down normally.

Note: The item still needs decayMinutes for decay speed.

SpawnComponents type names

SpawnComponents strings need to be resolved with Type.GetType(...). Plain names like nameof(SomeScript) usually will not resolve for a MonoBehaviour declared inside your plugin DLL, so CUCoreLib skips that component.

ItemRegistry.Register(
    "ToggleBlade",
    new CustomItemInfo
    {
        fullName = "Toggle blade",
        category = "weapon",
        SpawnComponents = new List<string>
        {
            "YourNamespace.ToggleBladeScript, YourModDll"
        }
    },
    icon
);

Use the full type name plus the assembly name without the .dll extension. Example: if the class is TestModd.ToggleBladeScript inside TestModd.dll, register "TestModd.ToggleBladeScript, TestModd".

Current runtime note: if Type.GetType(...) returns null, ApplyCustomSpawnComponents silently skips that entry, so a bad string will fail with no warning.

Custom data

CustomData is for your own mod's metadata: tuning values, upgrade tiers, internal labels, or small constants you want to keep near the registration. CUCoreLib stores those values on the registered CustomItemInfo and lets you read them back from spawned items.

ItemRegistry.Register(
    "laserdrill",
    new CustomItemInfo
    {
        fullName = "Laser drill",
        category = "tool",
        CustomData =
        {
            ["beamRange"] = 8f,
            ["warmupSeconds"] = 0.35f,
            ["upgradeFamily"] = "laserdrill"
        }
    },
    AssetLoader.LoadEmbeddedSprite("Images.laserdrill.png")
);

Retrieve it from the item with ItemRegistry.TryGetCustomData. The generic type is intentional: the dictionary stores object, so callers should ask for the type they expect.

if (ItemRegistry.TryGetCustomData<float>(item, "beamRange", out float beamRange))
{
    Logger.LogInfo($"Laser drill range: {beamRange}");
}

Consider adding a function to encapsulate the retrieval logic.

If the data changes per spawned item, do not store it in CustomData. Attach per-instance state to the Item with your own component.

Advanced items can use CustomItemInfo , which includes normal ItemInfo fields, vanilla LiquidItemInfo fields, and CUCoreLib-only fields like Container , Battery , Light , Tool , WornSprite , WornSpriteOffset , SpriteScaleDimensions , SpawnFrequency , and CustomData . Why is the mod doing it this way? Traditonally, the game sets these settings via the Unity prefab editor, and as such does not appear in the game's default item code. // Replace new ItemInfo{ ... } with new CustomItemInfo{ ... } Modded-only fields CustomItemInfo keeps vanilla fields like fullName , category , weight , tags , and useAction . It also exposes vanilla liquid-container fields like capacity , defaultContents , and autoFill . The fields below are CUCoreLib-only data. The base game does not know about them unless CUCoreLib reads them and applies the matching runtime behavior. Field Type What CUCoreLib uses it for ID string Filled by ItemRegistry.Register from the separate ID argument. You normally do not set this yourself. Icon Sprite Inventory/world sprite used by custom templates and cached under the item ID. Passing the sprite as the third register argument sets this for you. WornSprite Sprite Optional sprite applied while a wearable custom item is worn, then restored to Icon when dropped. WornSpriteOffset Vector2 Optional local-space offset applied to the worn item sprite after vanilla attaches it to desiredWearLimb . SpriteScale float Scale applied to the generated runtime template. Keep this near 1f unless your art was made at a different size. SpriteScaleDimensions SpriteScaleDimensions Scales the sprite toward a target pixel size like (14f, 14f) . Add true as the third tuple value to stop once either axis reaches the requested size instead of forcing both axes to meet it. SpawnFrequency int Loot pool injection weight. 0 means craft-only, 1 is the normal default, higher values make it more common. Container ContainerProperties Adds/configures a vanilla Container component on spawned items. Battery BatteryProperties Adds/configures a vanilla BatteryItem component on spawned items. Light LightProperties Adds/configures a Light2D emitter and optionally wires it to vanilla LightItem behavior. Bandage BandageProperties Installs a vanilla-style BandageMinigame limb action and applies limb healing, pain reduction, and bandage slow values. Syringe SyringeProperties Adds syringe-style liquid injection behavior through WaterContainerItem and SyringeMinigame . Tool ToolProperties Builds a vanilla AttackInfo and calls Body.Attack for melee-style tools or weapons. SpawnComponents List<string> Qualified MonoBehaviour type names CUCoreLib adds to the spawned item GameObject the first time the item appears. For plugin-defined scripts, use the assembly-qualified form like "YourNamespace.YourClass, YourModDll" . CustomData Dictionary<string, object> Registration-time metadata for your own mod code. Read it later with ItemRegistry.TryGetCustomData<T> . LiquidItemInfo fields Use these direct fields for normal liquid containers like bottles, cans, canteens, pouches, and drinkable items. Use SyringeProperties only when you want the syringe minigame and injection action. Field Type What it does capacity float Maximum liquid capacity in mL. defaultContents List<LiquidStack> Liquids added to freshly spawned items. autoFill bool Whether a loose item can fill itself from world liquid tiles when it has space. ContainerProperties Field Type What it does Capacity float Maximum total held weight for the vanilla Container . MaxWeightPerItem float Maximum weight any one contained item may have. EncumbranceReduction float Multiplier for how much contained weight counts against the player. 1f is normal; 0.5f feels half as heavy. StartCharge float Starting charge amount used to initialize item condition on fresh custom items. Preset BatteryItem.BatteryPreset Vanilla preset for accepted battery size: Small , Medium , or Large . BatteryType string Item ID for the battery installed by default, such as smallbattery , mediumbattery , or largebattery . SpawnWithBattery bool If true, fresh items start with BatteryType already loaded. If false, the item starts empty. BandageProperties Field Type What it does Effectiveness float Divisor for minigame use amount. Higher values make the item last longer. SkinHealAmount float Skin healing applied per use amount. BandageSlowAmount float Bleeding slowdown applied per use amount. PainReduction float Pain removed per use amount. BoneHealTimerReduction float Broken-bone recovery timer reduction per use amount. DislocationTimerReduction float Dislocation timer reduction per use amount. MinigameColor Color Color passed into the vanilla bandage minigame. CreateWrapSprite bool If true, CUCoreLib creates a temporary limb wrap sprite when used. WrapSpritePath string Resource path for the temporary wrap sprite. WrapSpriteColor Color Tint for the temporary wrap sprite. SyringeProperties This is for syringe-style limb injection, not general bottle/canteen liquid storage. Field Type What it does Capacity float Maximum liquid capacity in mL. AutoFill bool Whether the added WaterContainerItem auto-fills when supported by vanilla behavior. AmountPerFullUse float Liquid amount injected when the minigame multiplier is 1f . UseAverageColor bool If true, the minigame color comes from the contained liquid average. MinigameColor Color Fallback minigame color when UseAverageColor is false. DefaultContents List<LiquidStack> Liquids added to fresh spawned items. ToolProperties Field Type What it does Damage float Damage against animals or living targets. StructuralDamage float Damage against blocks, buildings, and non-animal structures. AttackCooldownMultiplier float Multiplier applied to vanilla attack cooldown after hitting animals. Distance float Raycast reach for the attack. KnockBack float Impulse applied through vanilla attack knockback. Cooldown float Base attack cooldown. AttackAnimation string Resource name for the swing animation prefab, usually SwingAnim . StaminaUse float Stamina spent when swinging. Piercing bool If true, vanilla attack raycasts can continue through hits. SwingSounds string[] Sound IDs randomly chosen when swinging. Volume float Swing sound volume. RotateAmount float Visual attack rotation amount. PhysicalSwing bool If true, vanilla arm power, exertion, and physical swing effects apply. DoAttackAnimation bool If true, vanilla arms swing animation plays. MetalMoreDamage bool If true, vanilla metallic target bonus damage applies. ConditionLossOnHit float Item condition subtracted after a successful hit. LightProperties Field Type What it does Intensity float Brightness of the generated Light2D . Color Color Light tint. Defaults to white. PointLightOuterRadius float Outer radius for point lights. PointLightInnerRadius float Inner radius for point lights. LightType CustomLightType CUCoreLib light type mapped internally to URP Light2D.LightType . Use CustomLightType.Point for lantern-style item light. Offset Vector2 Local offset for the light object relative to the item. AddLightItem bool If true, CUCoreLib wires the light through vanilla LightItem so container/on-off behavior can apply. Use this when the item itself stores charge or accepts battery behavior; use vanilla BatteryInfo when you are registering an actual battery item. ItemRegistry.Register("glassshiv", new CustomItemInfo { fullName = "Glass shiv", description = "A sharpened piece of glass wrapped in cloth.", category = "weapon", tags = "tool,cangetwet,cutting", weight = 0.25f, value = 4, rec = new Recognition(3), Tool = new ToolProperties { Damage = 22f, StructuralDamage = 8f, AttackCooldownMultiplier = 0.7f, Distance = 4.6f, KnockBack = 120f, Cooldown = 0.28f, StaminaUse = 0.35f, Piercing = true, ConditionLossOnHit = 0.08f } }, AssetLoader.LoadEmbeddedSprite("Images.glassshiv.png"), 1); Equippables Wearables are regular items with wearable fields set. wearSlotId is the save/load and replacement key. desiredWearLimb points the visual/armor behavior at a body region, while wearableArmor and wearableIsolation affect protection and temperature. ItemRegistry.Register( "fieldpack", new CustomItemInfo { fullName = "Field Pack", description = "A versatile pack for carrying supplies.", category = "tool", wearable = true, wearableCanBeHeld = true, desiredWearLimb = "UpTorso", wearSlotId = "back", wearableArmor = 0.18f, wearableIsolation = 0.08f, wearableHitDurabilityLossMultiplier = 0.7f, weight = 1.1f, value = 14, tags = "cangetwet", rec = new Recognition(4), WornSprite = AssetLoader.LoadEmbeddedSprite("Images.fieldpack-worn.png"), WornSpriteOffset = new Vector2(0f, -0.04f), Container = new ContainerProperties { Capacity = 8f, MaxWeightPerItem = 3f, EncumbranceReduction = 0.5f }, SpawnFrequency = 1 }, AssetLoader.LoadEmbeddedSprite("Images.fieldpack.png") ); In this example, we combined both the prior container tag with the new wearable fields to make a backpack that also functions as a container. A lot of more unique items such as this can be made with combinations of tags. Give it a try! Decay bit flags decayInfo is a byte, but it represents bit flags from ItemInfo.DecayType . Combine flags with bitwise OR (|). The enums are: NoDecayWithoutContainerItem (1) , NoDecayWhenNotWorn (2) , NoDecayWhenStill (4) , and BatteryDecay (16) . decayMinutes = 240f, decayInfo = (byte)( ItemInfo.DecayType.NoDecayWhenNotWorn | ItemInfo.DecayType.NoDecayWhenStill ); BatteryDecay (16) changes decay from normal item condition loss into battery drain. Use it for battery-powered items that should consume charge over time instead of rotting or breaking down normally. Note: The item still needs decayMinutes for decay speed. SpawnComponents type names SpawnComponents strings need to be resolved with Type.GetType(...) . Plain names like nameof(SomeScript) usually will not resolve for a MonoBehaviour declared inside your plugin DLL, so CUCoreLib skips that component. ItemRegistry.Register( "ToggleBlade", new CustomItemInfo { fullName = "Toggle blade", category = "weapon", SpawnComponents = new List<string> { "YourNamespace.ToggleBladeScript, YourModDll" } }, icon ); Use the full type name plus the assembly name without the .dll extension. Example: if the class is TestModd.ToggleBladeScript inside TestModd.dll , register "TestModd.ToggleBladeScript, TestModd" . Current runtime note: if Type.GetType(...) returns null , ApplyCustomSpawnComponents silently skips that entry, so a bad string will fail with no warning. Custom data CustomData is for your own mod's metadata: tuning values, upgrade tiers, internal labels, or small constants you want to keep near the registration. CUCoreLib stores those values on the registered CustomItemInfo and lets you read them back from spawned items. ItemRegistry.Register( "laserdrill", new CustomItemInfo { fullName = "Laser drill", category = "tool", CustomData = { ["beamRange"] = 8f, ["warmupSeconds"] = 0.35f, ["upgradeFamily"] = "laserdrill" } }, AssetLoader.LoadEmbeddedSprite("Images.laserdrill.png") ); Retrieve it from the item with ItemRegistry.TryGetCustomData . The generic type is intentional: the dictionary stores object , so callers should ask for the type they expect. if (ItemRegistry.TryGetCustomData<float>(item, "beamRange", out float beamRange)) { Logger.LogInfo($"Laser drill range: {beamRange}"); } Consider adding a function to encapsulate the retrieval logic. If the data changes per spawned item, do not store it in CustomData . Attach per-instance state to the Item with your own component.
